scope: Organic and natural-farming disease/pest triage for a prototype. Verify high-risk treatment locally.
organicPrinciples: Use clean seed, crop rotation, resistant/local varieties, and field sanitation first.
Prefer biological and botanical controls before any stronger intervention.
Avoid spraying during peak sun, strong wind, rain, or when beneficial insects are active.
Do not mix unknown remedies. Test on a small patch before wider application.
Escalate fast-spreading disease, severe wilting, unknown pests, or livestock/food safety risks to a local agriculture officer or KVK.

commonIssues.label: Fungal leaf spot / blight
visibleSigns: spots spreading after humidity or rain
organicRemedies: Remove heavily infected leaves and avoid leaving diseased residue in the field.
Improve spacing and air flow; avoid overhead irrigation late in the day.
Use a locally recommended bio-fungicide such as Trichoderma-based compost treatment where available.
commonIssues.prevention: Rotate crops and use clean seed. Avoid excess nitrogen and waterlogging.

commonIssues.label: Powdery mildew
organicRemedies: Remove the most affected leaves if infection is early and localized.
Improve sunlight and air movement around plants.
Use locally approved sulfur-free or bio-control guidance from the agriculture office for organic plots.
commonIssues.prevention: Avoid dense sowing and repeated susceptible crops in the same plot.

commonIssues.label: Aphids or sucking pests
organicRemedies: Spray plain water on a small affected patch to dislodge early clusters.
Encourage ladybird beetles and avoid broad-spectrum sprays.
Use neem-based botanical spray only at locally recommended dilution and timing.
commonIssues.prevention: Monitor field edges weekly and avoid excess nitrogen that attracts soft-bodied pests.

commonIssues.label: Nutrient or water stress
visibleSigns: wilting without clear spots or insects
organicRemedies: Check soil moisture at root depth before irrigation.
Apply mature compost or jeevamrit as locally practiced; avoid over-application.
Mulch where possible to reduce heat and moisture stress.
commonIssues.prevention: Use soil health card guidance, compost, mulch, and crop rotation with legumes.

safetyCopy.hinglish: Photo se final diagnosis pakka nahi hota. Agar rog tezi se fail raha hai, paudha murjha raha hai, ya nuksan zyada hai, local agriculture officer/KVK se turant confirm karein.
safetyCopy.hi: फोटो से अंतिम पहचान पक्की नहीं होती। अगर रोग तेजी से फैल रहा है, पौधा मुरझा रहा है या नुकसान ज्यादा है, तो स्थानीय कृषि अधिकारी/KVK से तुरंत पुष्टि करें।
safetyCopy.en: A photo is not a final diagnosis. If the issue is spreading fast, plants are wilting, or damage is high, confirm urgently with a local agriculture officer or KVK.
